page: Microsoft Azure D96s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the DSv5 server family. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz, this instance features 96 virtual CPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor and utilizes the x86_64 architecture. It does not include local storage or onboard GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable for external disk attachments.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core performance and memory bandwidth. The multi-core CPU performance, measured via stress-ng and Geekbench, falls into the top 25% tier, as does the multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Memory bandwidth tests show high throughput, with uncached read speeds reaching 323.4 GB/sec and write speeds at 131.45 GB/sec. Cryptographic capabilities are particularly notable, with the Passmark CPU encryption test ranking in the top 10% tier. Single-core performance, however, remains average, as reflected in the single-threaded stress-ng and Geekbench scores.

Given its balanced resource allocation and high multi-core throughput, the D96s_v5 is suited for multi-threaded workloads such as database operations, software compilation, and static web serving. It also demonstrates strong performance in prompt processing for small to medium language models, though text generation for large 70B models is weak due to the lack of GPU acceleration. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers a high density of dedicated compute resources and memory bandwidth without the added cost of local storage or specialized GPU hardware, making it a viable option for enterprise-scale general-purpose applications.
description: The Microsoft Azure D96s_v5 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the DSv5 family, featuring 96 vCPUs and 384.0 GB of RAM. Powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz, this x86_64 architecture instance provides 48 physical cores with hyper-threading. It does not include local storage or GPUs but is Premium Storage capable. Benchmarks reveal strong multi-core CPU performance, high memory bandwidth, and top-tier cryptographic encryption capabilities, while single-core performance remains average. The instance is well-suited for multi-threaded workloads, including database operations, software compilation, and static web serving. It offers a resource-dense, balanced profile for enterprise applications requiring substantial compute and memory without local storage overhead.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v5 is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance, ideal for database operations and software compilation.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96s_v5 features 96 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and strong multi-core performance for enterprise database and web workloads.
tagline: A high-density, general-purpose virtual machine offering ninety-six dedicated vCPUs and balanced memory for demanding multi-threaded enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 96 dedicated vCPUs and 384 GB of RAM on an Intel Xeon 8370C processor
Strong multi-core performance and high memory bandwidth in benchmark testing
Top-tier cryptographic performance and strong database operation throughput
Premium Storage capable with no local storage or onboard GPUs
Optimized for multi-threaded workloads, software compilation, and web serving
